nodes.name: Generate correlation_id
nodes.notes: Step 2: generate correlation_id (UUID) BEFORE any Attio call so retries are idempotent. Also normalizes form fields and derives the merchant SERVER-SIDE (the customer never picks it). Sets parcel_value = merchant ceiling (the value submitted to Evri), kept separate from cost_value (customer-stated).

nodes.name: Evri only?
nodes.notes: Phase 1 gate, BEFORE any Attio write. Only Evri claims proceed. Non-Evri couriers are rejected immediately with no records created. true -> Upsert Person; false -> Respond: rejected (non-Evri).

nodes.name: Upsert Person
nodes.notes: Step 3 (Evri only): PUT /v2/objects/people/records, matching on email_addresses. Returns the person record id at data.id.record_id.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { data: { values: { name: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.name, email_addresses: [$('Generate correlation_id').item.json.email] } } } }}

nodes.name: Create Claim
nodes.notes: Step 5 (Evri only): POST /v2/objects/claims/records. Links customer (people) + merchant (companies), status New, courier Evri. parcel_value = ceiling submitted to Evri; cost_value = customer-stated. Returns claim record id at data.id.record_id.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { data: { values: { correlation_id: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.correlation_id, order_number: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.order_number, courier: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.courier, tracking_number: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.tracking_number, additional_tracking: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.additional_tracking, claim_type: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.claim_type, parcel_count: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.parcel_count, delivery_postcode: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.delivery_postcode, phone_number: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.phone, customer_comment: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.customer_comment, status: 'New', cost_value: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.cost_value, parcel_value: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.parcel_value, customer: [{ target_object: 'people', target_record_id: $('Upsert Person').item.json.data.id.record_id }] } } } }}

nodes.name: Upload Photo
nodes.notes: Step 6 (Evri only): POST /v2/files (multipart). Streams the webhook's binary photo, linked to the claim record id. NOTE: verify exact /v2/files field names against the current Attio API before going live (file-upload params change more often than record endpoints).

nodes.name: Process Claim (FastAPI)
nodes.notes: Step 7 (Evri only): POST {AGENT_BASE_URL or http://localhost:8000}/claims/{correlation_id}/process. Hands the claim to the FastAPI agent service (extract -> enrich -> decide -> generate -> dispatch). Idempotent on correlation_id. On n8n Cloud, set the base URL to your public agent URL.

nodes.name: Respond: processed
nodes.notes: Step 8: 200 + claim reference for processed Evri claims.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: true, correlation_id: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.correlation_id, claim_record_id: $('Create Claim').item.json.data.id.record_id, status: 'New', message: 'Claim captured and Evri processing started.' } }}

nodes.name: Respond: rejected (non-Evri)
nodes.notes: Phase 1: non-Evri couriers are rejected up front. No Attio records are created. Returns 422 with the reason.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: false, correlation_id: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.correlation_id, courier: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.courier, message: 'Rejected: only Evri claims are supported in Phase 1.' } }}

nodes.name: Form: claim-intake

parameters.formDescription: Submit a courier claim. Evri claims are processed automatically.

values.fieldLabel: What is the reason for your claim?

nodes.notes: Public intake form (responseNode mode -> the Respond to Webhook nodes return JSON). Fields arrive keyed by their labels; 'Generate correlation_id' maps them. Merchant is derived server-side, never collected here.
